name: ESSAI 2026
venue: European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence, Vienna, Austria
description: The 4th European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence (ESSAI) takes place in Vienna, Austria from 6 to 10 July 2026. It offers introductory and advanced AI courses, specialized tutorials, and focuses on the themes of Bilateral AI and Digital Humanism for PhD students, young researchers, and senior scholars. The program includes a rich social agenda, networking opportunities, and is co‑located with the 5th ACM Europe Digital Humanism Summer School.

name: OpenEuroLLM Winter School
description: The 2026 Winter School on Multilinguality in LLM Development and Evaluation is held at the Skeikampen conference hotel north of Oslo, Norway. It targets NLP researchers, especially those from EuroHPC consortium countries, and offers doctoral training within the Circle U university alliance. The program features technical talks, hands‑on sessions, and discussions on multilingual large language models, open‑science practices, and evaluation challenges, complemented by networking opportunities and a scenic mountain setting.

name: Probabilistic AI School
description: The Probabilistic AI School 2026 will be held in Vilnius, Lithuania from August 3 to August 7, 2026. The school aims to provide an inclusive educational environment covering state-of-the-art machine learning and artificial intelligence topics for enthusiasts, students, academics, and industry professionals. It is co-organised with the Artificial Intelligence Association of Lithuania (AIAL) and continues the series' mission of fostering collaboration across the AI community.

name: BMVA Computer Vision Summer School
description: The 29th BMVA Computer Vision Summer School will be held at Durham University in Durham, England. It offers an intensive week of lectures and lab sessions covering a broad spectrum of computer‑vision topics, delivered by leading researchers from the UK and abroad. The programme is designed for graduate students and early‑career researchers seeking hands‑on experience and exposure to current advances in the field.

name: Oxford Machine Learning Summer School 2026
description: OxML 2026 offers a summer program to upskill participants in the latest AI theory and applications across three specialised tracks. Learners study with world‑renowned experts from frontier AI labs in both academia and industry, benefiting from a unique and customisable experience. Participants can choose any combination of tracks to align with their individual learning goals.

name: Robotics, Perception and Learning (RPL) Summer School
venue: KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm, Sweden
description: The RPL Summer School 2026 is a six‑day event for doctoral and postdoctoral researchers focusing on robotics, perception, and learning. It covers topics such as machine learning, computer vision, robotics, human‑robot interaction, planning, and decision making, and combines keynote talks, industry panels, and open brainstorming sessions. The school takes place at the Happy Tammsvik resort in Stockholm, offering a relaxed setting to foster new collaborations and concrete research ideas.

name: Machine Learning Crash Course at MALGA
description: The MLCC2026 Summer School – Machine Learning Crash Course – provides an introduction to fundamental machine‑learning concepts and algorithms. It is aimed at undergraduate and graduate students, as well as professionals, and is open to participants from any university after a selection process. The five‑day in‑person program in Genoa combines theoretical lectures with hands‑on lab sessions and includes a final test consisting of notebook work and a report.

name: UK Robotics Summer School
venue: National Robotarium, Heriot-Watt University, Edinburgh, United Kingdom
description: The UK Robotics Summer School 2026 is a five‑day program held in Edinburgh, United Kingdom, from 22 to 26 June 2026. It offers expert‑led lectures, tutorials and industry talks covering bio‑inspired robotics, healthcare robotics, generative AI for robotics, safe AI and real‑world applications, with hands‑on experience using state‑of‑the‑art technology. The school targets master’s‑level students and industry practitioners, providing networking opportunities with academics and industry leaders.

name: International Summer School for Advanced Studies on Biometrics, Behavior, and Vision
description: The 2026 Biometrics Summer School takes place in Alghero, Sardinia, Italy, hosted at the Hotel Dei Pini. It focuses on the impact of generative AI and large foundation models on biometric and human‑recognition technologies, covering machine learning, image understanding, signal analysis, neuroscience, robotics, forensic and digital forensics. The program is aimed at PhD students, post‑docs, researchers and professionals such as forensic examiners and police officers, offering intensive lectures by 20 international experts, hands‑on sessions, and optional video‑conference participation.

name: 1st Internationa School on the Cognitive, Ethical, and Societal Dimensions of AI - AIces
description: The AIces 2026 International School focuses on the cognitive, ethical and societal dimensions of artificial intelligence. It is aimed at graduate and postgraduate students as well as industry practitioners, offering parallel courses, a symposium, and open debates. The event takes place in Porto, Portugal from March 30 to April 2, 2026, with both face‑to‑face interaction and live online participation, and recordings available for 45 days after the school.

name: Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal (ELS) aspects of AI and AS at Umeå University
description: The Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal aspects of AI and Autonomous Systems brings together researchers and practitioners to discuss multidisciplinary challenges. It offers lectures, hands‑on training, a poster session and a social program to foster networking and professional exchange. The event takes place at Umeå University in Sweden from 11 to 13 March 2026 and is open to students, faculty and other participants, with a registration fee covering logistics and meals.

name: Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM 2026)
description: The Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM) takes place at the Escandille in Autrans, France. It aims to bring together researchers from fields such as computer vision, speech/audio processing, and human‑robot interaction to foster cross‑disciplinary exchange through formal courses and informal networking. Set in the scenic Vercors mountains near Grenoble, the venue also offers opportunities for outdoor activities and relaxed social interaction.

name: Machine Learning Summer School (MLSS) Melbourne
venue: Sandridge Events Centre, Port Melbourne, Melbourne, Australia
description: The 2026 Machine Learning Summer School (MLSS) takes place at the Sandridge Events Centre in Melbourne, Australia. It is a two‑week, in‑person program aimed at 50 hand‑picked PhD students and early‑career researchers, covering core theory and emerging topics beyond large language models. World‑class speakers from academia and industry deliver lectures and tutorials on a wide range of subjects, from deep learning and optimization to geometric deep learning and AI in industry.

name: Northern Lights Deep Learning Winter School
venue: UiT The Arctic University of Norway, Tromsø, Norway
description: The 2027 Winter School is part of the Northern Lights Deep Learning Conference and will be held in Tromsø, Norway. It offers intensive training and lectures on state-of-the-art deep learning methods, covering topics such as image classification, object detection, segmentation, time series prediction, and speech recognition. The school targets researchers and practitioners looking to deepen their expertise and foster collaborations within the international deep learning community.

name: Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS)
description: The Sixth Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS) is an 11‑day intensive program offering AI lectures, hands‑on labs, and project work. It targets a diverse audience that includes senior undergraduates, graduate students, PhD candidates, faculty members, and industry professionals, aiming to provide fundamentals as well as cutting‑edge AI topics. The school brings together over 500 in‑person participants and thousands of livestream viewers at Swostishree Gurukul in Kathmandu, fostering networking and collaboration across South Asia and the global AI community.

name: EEML Summer School
venue: Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School, Cetinje, Montenegro
description: The Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School takes place in Cetinje, Montenegro from 27 July to 1 August 2026. It offers a series of lectures and tutorials on modern machine‑learning research presented by experts from leading universities and industry labs. The program targets graduate students and early‑career researchers interested in advancing their knowledge and networking within the Eastern European AI community.

name: Mediterranean Summer School
description: The Mediterranean Machine Learning (M2L) Summer School is a five‑day program offering keynotes, lectures, and hands‑on sessions led by international AI experts. It is aimed at doctoral and outstanding master students, as well as academics and practitioners, especially from the Mediterranean region, and covers topics such as computer vision, natural language processing, generative models, reinforcement learning, and AI ethics. The school also provides networking opportunities through poster sessions, social activities, and informal interactions with speakers and sponsors.

name: Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI
venue: Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications, Rabat, Morocco
description: The AI Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI is a five‑day program held at the Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications in Rabat, Morocco. It targets master’s and Ph.D. students, post‑docs, and early‑career researchers, offering lectures, hands‑on labs, and project work on state‑of‑the‑art multimodal and generative AI techniques. The school also provides networking opportunities with AI leaders and industry partners to foster the next generation of AI talent in Morocco.

name: AthNLP Summer School 2026
description: The Athens Natural Language Processing Summer School 2026 will be held at the NCSR Demokritos campus in Athens, Greece. The program, organized by RC Athena, NCSR Demokritos, Athens University of Economics and Business and Heriot‑Watt University, offers intensive training in natural language processing techniques. It runs from 2 to 8 September 2026 and is linked to the 14th EETN Conference on Artificial Intelligence taking place shortly after.

name: Advanced Artificial Intelligence: GenAI & Deep Unsupervised Learning
venue: Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: The Advanced Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning summer school at Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford, focuses on generative AI and deep unsupervised learning techniques. It provides theoretical foundations and hands‑on experience with clustering, GANs, self‑supervised learning, and related models across real‑world applications. The programme is aimed at STEM students with intermediate AI/ML knowledge who are considering graduate study or research, and it is offered as a residential or online course.

name: AI4Science Summer School
description: The 2nd Summer School on Advancing Scientific Discovery with AI takes place in Caen, Normandy, France. It offers a comprehensive program covering machine learning for scientific data analysis, AI‑driven experimental design, generative models, and integration of AI with physical simulations, featuring hands‑on workshops and case studies led by leading experts. The school is aimed at PhD students, post‑doctoral researchers and scientists familiar with mathematical modelling, numerical computation and Python, providing them with skills to apply AI to accelerate scientific research.

name: LUT Summer School
description: LUT Summer School offers a one‑week intensive programme of courses on sustainability, climate action, marketing, management, and inventive thinking, combining business and technology topics. The programme is aimed at bachelor’s and master’s students from any university who wish to earn 3 ECTS credits while experiencing Finnish summer on the Lappeenranta campus. It features a mix of workshops, group work, and social activities such as sauna evenings and outdoor excursions, providing both academic depth and cultural immersion.

name: Machine Learning Crash Course at MALGA
description: The MLCC2026 Summer School, a Machine Learning Crash Course, takes place at the University of Genoa in Genoa, Italy. It offers an introductory, hands‑on program covering fundamental concepts, algorithms and practical lab sessions, aimed at undergraduate and graduate students as well as professionals. The course is held entirely in person at the DIBRIS/DIMA building, includes daily lectures and labs, and provides university credit upon successful completion.
